builderCode:
# ── Panels Demo ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# A Panel groups child elements and positions them relative to its top-left.
# Moving the panel shifts all its children together.
#
# Properties:
#   position    — (row, col) top-left corner of the panel on the grid
#   width, height — minimum size in cells; panel grows to fit children
#   show_border — True: dashed border, name label, and subtle background fill
#
# Children use positions relative to the panel's top-left (0, 0).
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Helper: add a Rect, Circle, and Arrow to a panel (or at an offset directly)
def place(panel=None, row_offset=0, col_offset=0):
    r = Rect  (position=(row_offset,     col_offset),     color=(239, 68,  68))
    c = Circle(position=(row_offset,     col_offset + 1), color=(59,  130, 246))
    a = Arrow (position=(row_offset + 1, col_offset),     color=(16,  185, 129), orientation='right')
    if panel:
        r.position = (0, 0)
        c.position = (0, 1)
        a.position = (1, 0)
        panel.add(r, c, a)

# ── 1. No panel ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Shapes placed directly on the grid at absolute positions.
Label(label='No panel', position=(1, 1))
place(row_offset=2, col_offset=1)

# ── 2. Inside a panel — no border ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# Same shapes inside a panel. Children use positions relative to the panel.
# With show_border=False (default) the container is invisible.
# Try changing p2.position — all three shapes move together.
Label(label='In a panel', position=(1, 8))
p2 = Panel()
p2.show_border = True
p2.position = (2, 7)
place(p2)

# ── 3. Border + background + minimum size ─────────────────────────────────────
# show_border=True draws a dashed border and a subtle background fill.
# width=5, height=4 sets a minimum: even with only 2×2 of shapes the panel
# occupies a 5×4 area — the background reveals the extra empty space.
Label(label='Border + min size  (5×4)', position=(6, 1), width=5)
p3 = Panel(name='container')
p3.position    = (7, 1)
p3.show_border = True
p3.width  = 5
p3.height = 4
p3.color = (100,100,50)
place(p3)

# ── 4. No border, no background ───────────────────────────────────────────────
# show_border=False (default) — the panel container is completely invisible.
# Useful for grouping shapes without any visual chrome.
Label(name='', label='No border', position=(6, 8))
p4 = Panel()
p4.position    = (7, 7)
p4.show_border = False
place(p4)
